ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic cheesecake entry (FOODON_00002473). No specific entry exists for cookie crumble cheesecake or a cheesecake-plus-beverage set. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers cheesecake as a category. No specific code exists for cookie crumble variant or a set meal including a beverage. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a commercial cheesecake-beverage set meal.

serving.description: 1 slice of cookie crumble cheesecake plus 1 tall beverage (approximately 354 ml)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of one slice of cookie crumble cheesecake (approximately 120 g) paired with one Starbucks tall-size beverage (approximately 310 g), totalling approximately 430 g.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 19270); supplemented with USDA Branded Food data for Starbucks tall latte (FDC ID 2103881) for the beverage component
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The cheesecake component is modelled on USDA SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared cheesecake (FDC ID 19270: ~321 kcal/100 g). The beverage component is modelled on a Starbucks tall 2% milk latte (USDA Branded, FDC ID 2103881: ~54 kcal/100 g). A weighted composite per-100 g value is derived assuming 120 g cheesecake and 310 g beverage in a 430 g serving. Composite per-100 g = (cheesecake_value × 120 + beverage_value × 310) / 430. This is an estimation; no Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry exists for this commercial set.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake GI analogous estimate based on: 'International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008' — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Cookie Crumble Cheesecake. GI of 46 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for commercially prepared cheesecake (GI ~46, low category), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The beverage component (milk-based latte) has a GI of approximately 30–40; the composite set GI is estimated at 46 (low), dominated by the cheesecake component. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (46 × 63.6 g carbs in 430 g serving) / 100 ≈ 29.3; however, using per-100 g carbohydrate of 14.8 g and a 430 g serving: available carbs = 63.6 g; GL = 46 × 63.6 / 100 = 29.3. Reported GL per serving = 29.3, rounded to 29.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and high sugar load
hypertension.guidance: This set meal provides approximately 421 mg sodium per serving; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to occasional treats and monitor total daily sodium intake, keeping it below 2000 mg per day as recommended by the Health Promotion Board Singapore.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar and refined carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: The set meal delivers approximately 63.6 g of carbohydrates per serving with a glycemic load of approximately 29, which is high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this item and opt for lower-carbohydrate, lower-sugar alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from cream cheese, butter, and whole milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving provides approximately 17.6 g of saturated fat, exceeding the recommended daily limit of 14–15 g for cardiovascular risk reduction; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this item or consume it only rarely as part of a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Cookie Crumble Cheesecake with Tall Beverage Set is a commercial dessert-and-drink combo sold at Starbucks Singapore, typically available from 2 PM daily at a promotional price of approximately SGD 14.90. The cheesecake component is a rich, baked New York-style cheesecake featuring a buttery digestive biscuit base, a dense and creamy cream cheese filling, and a generous topping of crumbled chocolate or vanilla cookies, providing a contrast of smooth and crunchy textures. The tall beverage component is a standard Starbucks tall-size drink (approximately 354 ml), typically a hot or iced latte, Americano, or tea, depending on customer choice. Nutritionally, the set is calorie-dense, providing an estimated 570–600 kcal per serving, with significant saturated fat from cream cheese and butter, and a high sugar content from the cookie crumble topping and sweetened beverage. The glycemic index of the cheesecake component is estimated at approximately 46 (low GI), but the glycemic load per full serving is high at approximately 29 due to the large carbohydrate portion. Clinically, this set is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or hypertension, and should be consumed only occasionally. It is vegetarian and Halal-certified at Starbucks Singapore outlets.

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded entry exists for this specific Starbucks Singapore set meal. Nutrient values are derived from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy commercially prepared cheesecake (FDC ID 19270) and USDA Branded Starbucks tall latte data, adapted to the estimated 120 g cheesecake plus 310 g beverage serving composition. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for commercially prepared cheesecake. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake source entry (FDC ID 19270) at a level that can be reliably composited. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA source for this food category. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake entry used for this estimation. 4. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the cited USDA sources for this food type. 5. The glycemic_load_per_serving of 29.3 is calculated as (GI 46 × available carbohydrate 63.6 g per 430 g serving) / 100. 6. The serving size of 430 g is an estimate based on a standard Starbucks cheesecake slice (~120 g) plus a tall beverage (~310 g liquid weight). 7. Starbucks Singapore is Halal-certified; is_halal is set to true accordingly. 8. This record is a composite estimation; a direct nutritional analysis of the Starbucks Singapore Cookie Crumble Cheesecake product would supersede these values. 9. The Hindi alias 'कुकी क्रम्बल चीज़केक और टॉल ड्रिंक सेट' uses standard Devanagari script throughout.
